287	
288		reviewSummary := EventBodySummary{
289			Harness:     reviewRes.ReviewerHarness,
290			Model:       reviewRes.ReviewerModel,
291			InputBytes:  reviewRes.InputBytes,
292			OutputBytes: reviewRes.OutputBytes,
293			ElapsedMS:   reviewRes.DurationMS,
294		}
295		reviewBudgetExceeded := chargeReviewCost()
296		switch reviewRes.Verdict {
297		case VerdictApprove:
298			// Approved: record the verdict event and then close. The close must
299			// land AFTER the review event so the gate sees the terminal verdict.
300			_ = in.Store.AppendEvent(in.Bead.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
301				Kind:      "review",
302				Summary:   "APPROVE",
303				Body:      AppendEventSummary(ReviewEventBody("APPROVE", reviewRes.Rationale, artifactPath), reviewSummary),
304				Actor:     in.Assignee,
305				Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
306				CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
307			})
308			if reviewBudgetExceeded {
309				// Reviewer cost tripped the budget cap; leave bead open so the
310				// loop's BudgetStop can stop the drain before the next claim.
311				out.Report = report
312				out.Approved = false
313				return out
314			}
315			if cerr := in.Store.CloseWithEvidence(in.Bead.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); cerr != nil {
316				out.Report = report
317				out.StoreErrOp = "CloseWithEvidence"
318				out.StoreErr = cerr
319				return out
320			}
321		case VerdictRequestChanges:
322			_ = in.Store.AppendEvent(in.Bead.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
323				Kind:      "review",
324				Summary:   "REQUEST_CHANGES",
325				Body:      AppendEventSummary(ReviewEventBody("REQUEST_CHANGES", reviewRes.Rationale, artifactPath), reviewSummary),
326				Actor:     in.Assignee,
327				Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
328				CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
329			})
330			report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges
331			report.Detail = "pre-close review: REQUEST_CHANGES"
332			out.Approved = false
333		case VerdictBlock:
334			rationale := strings.TrimSpace(reviewRes.Rationale)
335			classification := ClassifyReviewFindings(reviewRes)
336			if rationale == "" && len(classification.Evidence) == 0 {
337				_ = in.Store.AppendEvent(in.Bead.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
338					Kind:      "review-malfunction",
339					Summary:   "BLOCK without rationale",
340					Body:      AppendEventSummary(ReviewEventBody("BLOCK without rationale", "", artifactPath), reviewSummary),
341					Actor:     in.Assignee,
342					Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
343					CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
344				})
345				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction
346				report.Detail = "pre-close review: malformed BLOCK verdict (missing rationale)"
347				report.ReviewRationale = ""
348				out.Report = report
349				out.Approved = false
350				return out
351			}
352			_ = in.Store.AppendEvent(in.Bead.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
353				Kind:      "review",
354				Summary:   "BLOCK",
355				Body:      AppendEventSummary(rationale, reviewSummary),
356				Actor:     in.Assignee,
357				Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
358				CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
359			})
360			if classification.Class == ReviewFindingClassMalfunction {
361				_ = in.Store.AppendEvent(in.Bead.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
362					Kind:      "review-malfunction",
363					Summary:   ReviewFindingClassMalfunction,
364					Body:      AppendEventSummary(classification.Reason, reviewSummary),
365					Actor:     in.Assignee,
366					Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
367					CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
368				})
369				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction
370				report.Detail = "pre-close review: " + ReviewFindingClassMalfunction
371				out.Report = report
372				out.Approved = false
373				return out
374			}
375			if terminalClass := classifyTerminalReviewBlock(reviewRes); terminalClass != "" {
376				applyTerminalReviewBlock(in.Store, in.Bead.ID, in.Assignee, now().UTC(), terminalClass, report.ResultRev)
377				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewTerminalBlock
378				report.Detail = "pre-close review: terminal " + terminalClass
379				out.Report = report
380				out.Approved = false
381				return out
382			}
383			// Non-terminal BLOCK: schedule one bounded repair cycle (review_fixable_gap).
384			// If a repair cycle has already been scheduled for this result_rev, fall
385			// through to the regular BLOCK triage path so the policy cannot loop.
386			if classification.AutomatedRepairEligible && !hasReviewFixableGapRepairScheduled(in.Store, in.Bead.ID, report.ResultRev) {
387				groupID := ""
388				if reviewGroup != nil {
389					groupID = reviewGroup.Bundle.GroupID
390				}
391				repairCtx := scheduleReviewFixableGapRepair(
392					in.Store, in.Bead.ID, in.Assignee, now().UTC(),
393					groupID, report.ResultRev,
394					strings.TrimSpace(reviewRes.Rationale),
395					report.ActualPower,
396				)
397				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewFixableGap
398				report.Detail = "pre-close review: review_fixable_gap"
399				out.Report = report
400				out.Approved = false
401				out.RepairContext = repairCtx
402				return out
403			}
404			report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock
405			report.Detail = "pre-close review: BLOCK (flagged for human)"
406			out.Approved = false
407		}
408		if reviewRes.Verdict == VerdictBlock || reviewRes.Verdict == VerdictRequestChanges {
409			_ = applyReviewTriageDecision(in.Store, in.Bead.ID, in.Assignee, now().UTC(), report.Tier)
410		}
411	
412		out.Report = report
413		return out
414	}
415	
416	func runPreCloseReviewGroup(ctx context.Context, reviewer BeadReviewer, beadID, resultRev string, impl ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewGroupResult, error) {
417		if reviewer == nil {
418			return nil, fmt.Errorf("review-group: reviewer required")
419		}
420		if groupReviewer, ok := reviewer.(reviewGroupReviewer); ok {
421			return groupReviewer.ReviewGroup(ctx, beadID, resultRev, impl)
422		}
423		result, err := reviewer.ReviewBead(ctx, beadID, resultRev, impl)
424		group := &ReviewGroupResult{
425			BeadID:    beadID,
426			ResultRev: resultRev,
427			Slots: []ReviewGroupSlotResult{
428				{
429					ReviewerIndex: 0,
430					Result:        result,
431				},
432			},
433		}
434		if err != nil {
435			group.Slots[0].Error = err.Error()
436		}
437		return group, err
438	}
439	
440	func reviewErrorReviewerIndex(group *ReviewGroupResult, res *ReviewResult) (int, bool) {
441		if group == nil || len(group.Slots) < 2 {
442			return 0, false
443		}
444		if res != nil {
445			for _, slot := range group.Slots {
446				if slot.Result == res {
447					return slot.ReviewerIndex, true
448				}
449			}
450			return res.ReviewerIndex, true
451		}
452		for _, slot := range group.Slots {
453			if slot.Error != "" {
454				return slot.ReviewerIndex, true
455			}
456		}
457		return 0, true
458	}
459	
460	func reducePreCloseReviewGroup(group *ReviewGroupResult) (*ReviewResult, error) {
461		if group == nil || len(group.Slots) == 0 {
462			return nil, fmt.Errorf("review-group: no review slots")
463		}
464		var firstApprove *ReviewResult
465		var firstNonApprove *ReviewResult
466		var firstResult *ReviewResult
467		for _, slot := range group.Slots {
468			if slot.Result != nil && firstResult == nil {
469				firstResult = slot.Result
470			}
471			if slot.Error != "" && slot.Result == nil {
472				if firstResult != nil {
473					return firstResult, fmt.Errorf("review-group slot %d: %s", slot.ReviewerIndex, slot.Error)
474				}
475				return nil, fmt.Errorf("review-group slot %d: %s", slot.ReviewerIndex, slot.Error)
476			}
477			if slot.Result == nil {
478				if firstResult != nil {
479					return firstResult, fmt.Errorf("review-group slot %d: missing review result", slot.ReviewerIndex)
480				}
481				return nil, fmt.Errorf("review-group slot %d: missing review result", slot.ReviewerIndex)
482			}
483			res := slot.Result
484			switch res.Verdict {
485			case VerdictApprove:
486				if !reviewResultHasACEvidence(res) {
487					return res, fmt.Errorf("%w: approve verdict without per-AC evidence", ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable)
488				}
489				if firstApprove == nil {
490					firstApprove = res
491				}
492			case VerdictRequestChanges, VerdictBlock:
493				if firstNonApprove == nil {
494					firstNonApprove = res
495				}
496			default:
497				if firstNonApprove == nil {
498					firstNonApprove = res
499				}
500			}
501		}
502		if firstNonApprove != nil {
503			return firstNonApprove, nil
504		}
505		return firstApprove, nil
506	}
507	
508	func reviewResultHasACEvidence(res *ReviewResult) bool {
509		if res == nil || res.Verdict != VerdictApprove {
510			return false
511		}
512		if len(res.PerAC) == 0 {
513			return false
514		}
515		for _, ac := range res.PerAC {
516			if strings.TrimSpace(ac.Evidence) == "" {
517				return false
518			}
519		}
520		return true
521	}
522	
523	// applyReviewTriageDecision runs the triage policy after a BLOCK or
524	// REQUEST_CHANGES review verdict has been recorded for `beadID`. It reads
525	// prior review events to compute the BLOCK count, biases the result toward
526	// re-attempt when the latest BLOCK was paired with a degraded reviewer
527	// (kind:review-pairing-degraded), then applies the chosen action by writing
528	// metadata, labels, and a kind:triage-decision event.
529	//
530	// Errors from the underlying store are best-effort: callers continue
531	// regardless so a triage-side failure cannot strand a bead in an
532	// inconsistent review state. This callout previously lived as a method on
533	// ExecuteBeadWorker; the C3
534	// extraction (ddx-a921ff01) moves it inside the post-merge-review pipeline
535	// driven by RunPostMergeReview / try.Attempt.
536	func applyReviewTriageDecision(store ExecuteBeadLoopStore, beadID, actor string, now time.Time, currentTier string) error {
537		events, err := store.Events(beadID)
538		if err != nil {
539			return err
540		}
541	
542		var blockTimestamps []time.Time
543		var pairingDegraded []time.Time
544		for _, ev := range events {
545			switch {
546			case ev.Kind == "review" && strings.TrimSpace(ev.Summary) == "BLOCK":
547				blockTimestamps = append(blockTimestamps, ev.CreatedAt)
548			case ev.Kind == ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind:
549				pairingDegraded = append(pairingDegraded, ev.CreatedAt)
550			}
551		}
552	
553		// History excludes the current attempt. The just-appended BLOCK (if any)
554		// is the last entry in blockTimestamps and must not be counted as prior
555		// history per triage.Decide()'s contract.
556		historyCount := len(blockTimestamps)
557		if historyCount > 0 {
558			historyCount--
559		}
560		history := make([]triage.FailureMode, 0, historyCount)
561		for i := 0; i < historyCount; i++ {
562			history = append(history, triage.FailureModeReviewBlock)
563		}
564	
565		policy := triage.DefaultPolicy()
566		action := policy.Decide(beadID, triage.FailureModeReviewBlock, history)
567	
568		pairedDegraded := latestBlockPairedDegraded(blockTimestamps, pairingDegraded)
569		if pairedDegraded && action == triage.ActionEscalateTier {
570			action = triage.ActionReAttemptWithContext
571		}
572	
573		return applyTriageAction(store, beadID, actor, now, action, currentTier, pairedDegraded)
574	}
575	
576	// latestBlockPairedDegraded reports whether the most recent BLOCK event was
577	// paired with a review-pairing-degraded event from the same attempt window
578	// (between the previous BLOCK and this one).
579	func latestBlockPairedDegraded(blocks, pairing []time.Time) bool {
580		if len(blocks) == 0 || len(pairing) == 0 {
581			return false
582		}
583		latest := blocks[len(blocks)-1]
584		var prev time.Time
585		if len(blocks) >= 2 {
586			prev = blocks[len(blocks)-2]
587		}
588		for _, t := range pairing {
589			if t.After(prev) && !t.After(latest) {
590				return true
591			}
592		}
593		return false
594	}
595	
596	// applyTriageAction performs the side effects for a chosen Action: writes a
597	// tier-pin hint into bead.Extra (escalate_tier), moves the bead to proposed
598	// for operator-required outcomes, and always records a triage-decision event.
599	func applyTriageAction(store ExecuteBeadLoopStore, beadID, actor string, now time.Time, action triage.Action, currentTier string, pairedDegraded bool) error {
600		body := map[string]any{
601			"action": string(action),
602			"mode":   string(triage.FailureModeReviewBlock),
603		}
604		if pairedDegraded {
605			body["pairing_degraded"] = true
606		}
607	
608		switch action {
609		case triage.ActionEscalateTier:
610			nextTier := nextEscalatedTier(currentTier)
611			body["tier_hint"] = string(nextTier)
612			_ = store.Update(beadID, func(b *bead.Bead) {
613				if b.Extra == nil {
614					b.Extra = make(map[string]any)
615				}
616				b.Extra[TriageTierHintKey] = string(nextTier)
617			})
618		case triage.ActionOperatorRequired:
619			if err := store.ParkToProposed(beadID, bead.ParkPostReviewMalfunction, func(b *bead.Bead) {
620				if b.Extra == nil {
621					b.Extra = make(map[string]any)
622				}
623				delete(b.Extra, TriageTierHintKey)
624				// Migration-only cleanup: defensive removal for legacy rows that escaped
625				// the lifecycle migration or arrived via external import.
626				b.Labels = removeBeadLabels(b.Labels, TriageNeedsHumanLabel, bead.LabelNeedsHuman, bead.LabelNeedsInvestigation)
627				clearReviewTriageClaimMetadata(b)
628				bead.SetNeedsHumanMeta(b, bead.NeedsHumanMeta{
629					Reason:          "review BLOCK triage reached operator-required rung",
630					Since:           now.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
631					Source:          "ddx agent execute-loop",
632					SuggestedAction: "review the blocked attempt and accept, split, block, or cancel",
633					Summary:         "review BLOCK triage requires operator decision",
634				})
635			}); err != nil {
636				return err
637			}
638		}
639	
640		bodyJSON, _ := json.Marshal(body)
641		return store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
642			Kind:      triageDecisionEventKind,
643			Summary:   string(triage.FailureModeReviewBlock) + ": " + string(action),
644			Body:      string(bodyJSON),
645			Actor:     actor,
646			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
647			CreatedAt: now,
648		})
649	}
650	
651	func clearReviewTriageClaimMetadata(b *bead.Bead) {
652		b.Owner = ""
653		if b.Extra == nil {
654			return
655		}
656		delete(b.Extra, "claimed-at")
657		delete(b.Extra, "claimed-pid")
658		delete(b.Extra, "claimed-machine")
659		delete(b.Extra, "claimed-session")
660		delete(b.Extra, "claimed-worktree")
661		delete(b.Extra, "execute-loop-heartbeat-at")
662	}
663	
664	// nextEscalatedTier returns the next tier above `current`. An unrecognised or
665	// empty current tier defaults to standard; smart is its own ceiling.
666	func nextEscalatedTier(current string) escalation.ModelTier {
667		switch escalation.ModelTier(current) {
668		case escalation.TierCheap:
669			return escalation.TierStandard
670		case escalation.TierStandard:
671			return escalation.TierSmart
672		case escalation.TierSmart:
673			return escalation.TierSmart
674		default:
675			return escalation.TierStandard
676		}
677	}
678

1	package bead
2	
3	import "fmt"
4	
5	// ParkReason identifies the canonical reason for transitioning a bead to
6	// proposed status for operator attention. Each constant maps to a fixed
7	// (Reason, Source) pair used by ParkToProposed.
8	type ParkReason string
9	
10	const (
11		ParkIntakeRejection           ParkReason = "intake_rejection"
12		ParkNoChangesOperatorRequired ParkReason = "no_changes_operator_required"
13		ParkPostReviewMalfunction     ParkReason = "post_review_malfunction"
14		ParkReviewTerminal            ParkReason = "review_terminal"
15		ParkConflictRecovery          ParkReason = "conflict_recovery"
16	)
17	
18	type parkReasonMeta struct {
19		Reason string
20		Source string
21	}
22	
23	var parkReasonMetaMap = map[ParkReason]parkReasonMeta{
24		ParkIntakeRejection:           {Reason: "pre-claim intake blocked execution", Source: "ddx agent execute-loop"},
25		ParkNoChangesOperatorRequired: {Reason: "operator decision required before another automated attempt", Source: "ddx agent execute-loop"},
26		ParkPostReviewMalfunction:     {Reason: "review BLOCK triage reached operator-required rung", Source: "ddx agent execute-loop"},
27		ParkReviewTerminal:            {Reason: "terminal review block requires operator decision", Source: "ddx agent execute-loop"},
28		ParkConflictRecovery:          {Reason: "land conflict requires operator judgment", Source: "ddx agent execute-loop"},
29	}
30	
31	// ParkToProposed transitions the bead to proposed status for operator
32	// attention. It enforces OperatorRequired=true and the canonical Reason and
33	// Source from the ParkReason mapping. The mutate callback runs after the
34	// status transition; pass nil if no additional metadata is needed.
35	func (s *Store) ParkToProposed(id string, reason ParkReason, mutate func(*Bead)) error {
36		meta, ok := parkReasonMetaMap[reason]
37		if !ok {
38			return fmt.Errorf("bead: unknown ParkReason %q", reason)
39		}
40		return s.TransitionLifecycle(id, StatusProposed, LifecycleTransitionOptions{
41			OperatorRequired: true,
42			Reason:           meta.Reason,
43			Source:           meta.Source,
44		}, func(b *Bead) error {
45			if mutate != nil {
46				mutate(b)
47			}
48			return nil
49		})
50	}

335		// ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictUnresolvable is set when execute-loop
336		// attempted 3-way ort auto-resolution and (if configured) a focused
337		// conflict-resolve agent run for a preserved iteration, and both failed.
338		// The bead is parked under LandConflictCooldown for operator review.
339		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictUnresolvable = "land_conflict_unresolvable"
340	
341		// ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictOperatorRequired is set when the focused
342		// conflict-resolve agent escalated to BLOCKING — the conflict requires
343		// operator judgment, not automated retry. The bead moves to status=proposed
344		// with a kind:land-conflict-operator-required event.
345		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictOperatorRequired = "land_conflict_operator_required"
346	
347		// ExecuteBeadStatusReviewTerminalBlock is set when a BLOCK review verdict
348		// is classified as a terminal operator-required condition —
349		// review_spec_gap, review_missing_acceptance, review_too_large at depth
350		// cap, or review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope. These classes do not re-enter
351		// the automated retry cycle and do not consume the no-progress retry
352		// budget; the bead moves to status=proposed until an operator
353		// intervenes.
354		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewTerminalBlock = "review_terminal_block"
355	
356		// ExecuteBeadStatusReviewFixableGap is set when a BLOCK review verdict is
357		// classified as a fixable gap — the issues are addressable by a repair
358		// attempt at higher MinPower with the reviewer's rationale as context.
359		// Exactly one repair cycle is scheduled per result_rev; a second
360		// review_fixable_gap on the same result_rev falls through to the regular
361		// BLOCK triage path instead.
362		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewFixableGap = "review_fixable_gap"
363	
364		// ExecuteBeadStatusRepairCycleExhausted is set when the candidate-cycle
365		// repair loop reaches its per-attempt repair_max_cycles limit before a
366		// reviewer approves the candidate. The latest candidate ref is retained for
367		// operator inspection.
368		ExecuteBeadStatusRepairCycleExhausted = "repair-cycle-exhausted"
369	)

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"strings"
8	)
9	
10	// ExecuteBeadIterationBlock is the machine-readable JSON payload embedded in
11	// execute-bead iteration commit messages as the "ddx-iteration:" trailer value.
12	// All fields are projected from result.json; the struct is the canonical source
13	// for both commit-message rendering and forward-compatibility testing.
14	type ExecuteBeadIterationBlock struct {
15		BeadID              string  `json:"bead_id"`
16		AttemptID           string  `json:"attempt_id"`
17		SessionID           string  `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
18		Harness             string  `json:"harness,omitempty"`
19		Provider            string  `json:"provider,omitempty"`
20		Model               string  `json:"model,omitempty"`
21		TotalTokens         int     `json:"total_tokens"`
22		CostUSD             float64 `json:"cost_usd"`
23		BaseRev             string  `json:"base_rev"`
24		ResultRev           string  `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
25		RequiredExecSummary string  `json:"required_exec_summary"`
26		Outcome             string  `json:"outcome"`
27		ExecutionBundle     string  `json:"execution_bundle"`
28	}
29	
30	// BuildIterationCommitMessage renders the canonical execute-bead iteration
31	// commit message from res. All values are projected from the result — the same
32	// fields that are written to result.json — so the message is re-derivable from
33	// the tracked artifact file without re-running the agent.
34	//
35	// The message includes:
36	//   - A structured subject line referencing the bead ID
37	//   - The ddx-iteration JSON block (compact agent/provider metadata)
38	//   - The canonical Git trailer set (Ddx-Attempt-Id, Ddx-Worker-Id,
39	//     Ddx-Harness, Ddx-Model, Ddx-Result-Status)
40	//
41	// Unknown fields are handled deterministically: ResultRev is omitted when
42	// empty; RequiredExecSummary defaults to "skipped" when unset; WorkerID falls
43	// back to SessionID when no distinct worker identity exists.
44	func BuildIterationCommitMessage(res *ExecuteBeadResult) string {
45		block := ExecuteBeadIterationBlock{
46			BeadID:              res.BeadID,
47			AttemptID:           res.AttemptID,
48			SessionID:           res.SessionID,
49			Harness:             res.Harness,
50			Provider:            res.Provider,
51			Model:               res.Model,
52			TotalTokens:         res.Tokens,
53			CostUSD:             res.CostUSD,
54			BaseRev:             res.BaseRev,
55			ResultRev:           res.ResultRev,
56			RequiredExecSummary: iterationRequiredExecSummary(res),
57			Outcome:             res.Outcome,
58			ExecutionBundle:     res.ExecutionDir,
59		}
60	
61		blockJSON, err := json.MarshalIndent(block, "", "  ")
62		if err != nil {
63			// Fallback: minimal inline block so the commit is never trailer-free.
64			blockJSON = []byte(fmt.Sprintf(`{"bead_id":%q,"attempt_id":%q}`, res.BeadID, res.AttemptID))
65		}
66	
67		// Ddx-Worker-Id falls back to SessionID when no distinct worker identity exists.
68		workerID := res.WorkerID
69		if workerID == "" {
70			workerID = res.SessionID
71		}
72	
73		var b strings.Builder
74		b.WriteString("chore: execute-bead iteration ")
75		b.WriteString(res.BeadID)
76		b.WriteString("\n\nddx-iteration: ")
77		b.WriteString(string(blockJSON))
78		// Blank line between the JSON block and the canonical trailer set is required
79		// by git-interpret-trailers so that the trailers are parsed correctly.
80		b.WriteString("\n\nDdx-Attempt-Id: ")
81		b.WriteString(res.AttemptID)
82		b.WriteString("\nDdx-Worker-Id: ")
83		b.WriteString(workerID)
84		b.WriteString("\nDdx-Harness: ")
85		b.WriteString(res.Harness)
86		b.WriteString("\nDdx-Model: ")
87		b.WriteString(res.Model)
88		b.WriteString("\nDdx-Result-Status: ")
89		b.WriteString(res.Status)
90		return b.String()
91	}
92	
93	// iterationRequiredExecSummary returns the required_exec_summary value for the
94	// ddx-iteration block. When the orchestrator has not yet evaluated gates (i.e.
95	// the preliminary result is written before the synthesized commit), the field
96	// defaults to "skipped" per the documented contract. After ApplyLandingToResult
97	// the field is populated with the real summary and the final result.json is
98	// written.
99	func iterationRequiredExecSummary(res *ExecuteBeadResult) string {
100		if res.RequiredExecSummary != "" {
101			return res.RequiredExecSummary
102		}
103		return "skipped"
104	}
105	
106	// BuildCommitMessageFromResultFile reads the result.json artifact at resultPath
107	// and renders the canonical execute-bead iteration commit message from it.
108	// This is the file-sourced rendering path: callers must write result.json before
109	// calling this function. The message is deterministic for a given result.json —
110	// anyone can re-derive it from the tracked bundle without re-running the agent.
111	func BuildCommitMessageFromResultFile(resultPath string) (string, error) {
112		raw, err := os.ReadFile(resultPath)
113		if err != nil {
114			return "", fmt.Errorf("reading result file %s: %w", resultPath, err)
115		}
116		var res ExecuteBeadResult
117		if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &res); err != nil {
118			return "", fmt.Errorf("parsing result file %s: %w", resultPath, err)
119		}
120		return BuildIterationCommitMessage(&res), nil
121	}
122

44		// merges the implementation commit. Populated by ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult.
45		LandedRev string `json:"landed_rev,omitempty"`

12	func TestReviewClassification_FixableGap(t *testing.T) {
13		got := ClassifyReviewFindings(&ReviewResult{
14			Verdict:   VerdictRequestChanges,
15			Rationale: "missing test coverage",
16			PerAC: []ReviewAC{
17				{
18					Number:   2,
19					Item:     "Add regression coverage",
20					Grade:    "REQUEST_CHANGES",
21					Evidence: "AC#2 regression test is missing in cli/internal/agent/foo_test.go",
22				},
23			},
24			Findings: []Finding{
25				{Severity: "warn", Summary: "Implementation is fixable: add the missing test.", Location: "cli/internal/agent/foo_test.go:42"},
26			},
27		})
28	
29		assert.Equal(t, ReviewFindingClassFixableGap, got.Class)
30		assert.True(t, got.AutomatedRepairEligible)
31		require.NotEmpty(t, got.Evidence)
32	}
33	
34	func TestReviewClassification_SpecGap(t *testing.T) {

36			Verdict: VerdictBlock,

39			},
40		})

43		assert.False(t, got.AutomatedRepairEligible)
44		require.NotEmpty(t, got.Evidence)
45	}

52			},
53		})
54	
55		assert.Equal(t, ReviewTerminalClassUnsafeOrOutScope, got.Class)
56		assert.False(t, got.AutomatedRepairEligible)
57		require.NotEmpty(t, got.Evidence)
58	}
59	
60	func TestReviewClassification_FreeformOnlyDoesNotRepair(t *testing.T) {
61		got := ClassifyReviewFindings(&ReviewResult{
62			Verdict:   VerdictBlock,
63			Rationale: "AC#3 is missing a test and can be repaired.",
64		})
65

70		store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
71		reviewer := beadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, _ string, _ ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewResult, error) {
72			return &ReviewResult{

76				ReviewerHarness: "claude",
77				ReviewerModel:   "claude-opus-4-6",
78				ResultRev:       "freeform01",
79			}, nil
80		})
81	
82		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
83		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
84	
85		out := RunPostMergeReview(context.Background(), PostMergeReviewInput{

98		})
99

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"

19		internalgit "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/git"
20		agentlib "github.com/easel/fizeau"
21	)
22	
23	// ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind is the kind:review-pairing-degraded event
24	// kind appended to the bead when the post-merge reviewer was routed to the
25	// same provider as the implementer. R4 (different reviewer) is best-effort
26	// emergent: bumping MinPower usually picks a different candidate, but the
27	// guarantee is not absolute. The event surfaces the degradation so operators
28	// can see when reviewer diversity collapsed.
29	const ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind = "review-pairing-degraded"
30	
31	// ReviewerEscalatedEventKind is the kind:reviewer-escalated event kind
32	// appended when the reviewer's MinPower is bumped above the baseline
33	// impl.ActualPower+1 due to prior review-error or review-pairing-degraded
34	// events for the same result_rev.
35	const ReviewerEscalatedEventKind = "reviewer-escalated"
36	
37	// ImplementerRouting captures the implementer's resolved routing details so
38	// the post-merge reviewer can build a paired ExecuteRequest with Role=reviewer,
39	// the same correlation, and MinPower bumped one above the implementer's
40	// actual selected power (R4 pairing).
41	type ImplementerRouting struct {
42		Harness     string
43		Provider    string
44		Model       string
45		ActualPower int
46		// Correlation is the implementer's correlation map. The reviewer copies
47		// its keys into its own dispatch metadata so events / session log /
48		// aggregations can join the two calls.
49		Correlation map[string]string
50	}
51	
52	// Verdict is the outcome of a post-merge bead review.
53	type Verdict string
54	
55	const (
56		// VerdictApprove means all AC items passed; the bead stays closed.
57		VerdictApprove Verdict = "APPROVE"
58		// VerdictRequestChanges means some AC items need fixing; the bead stays open
59		// and the pre-close gate records the verdict without closing.
60		VerdictRequestChanges Verdict = "REQUEST_CHANGES"
61		// VerdictBlock means escalation should stop; the bead is flagged for human
62		// review while remaining open.
63		VerdictBlock Verdict = "BLOCK"
64		// VerdictRequestClarification is used when the reviewer cannot adjudicate a
65		// needs-judgment AC without additional bead context. Routes to the operator
66		// lane (status=proposed with label) — does NOT block the drain queue further.
67		VerdictRequestClarification Verdict = "REQUEST_CLARIFICATION"
68	)
69	
70	// StrictnessMode controls how strictly the reviewer evaluates a bead.
71	// Derived from bead labels by BeadStrictnessMode.
72	type StrictnessMode string
73	
74	const (
75		// StrictnessStrict requires full AC verification including test names.
76		// Applied for kind:fix and kind:feat beads (default).
77		StrictnessStrict StrictnessMode = "strict"
78		// StrictnessBehaviorLight requires build-green and symbol/file evidence.
79		// Test-name match is only required when AC explicitly names a Test*.
80		// Applied for kind:refactor and kind:chore beads.
81		StrictnessBehaviorLight StrictnessMode = "behavior-light"
82		// StrictnessMechanical requires file/rename/symbol evidence only.
83		// Applied for kind:doc and kind:mechanical beads.
84		StrictnessMechanical StrictnessMode = "mechanical"
85	)
86	
87	// BeadStrictnessMode returns the reviewer strictness mode for a bead based on
88	// its labels. kind:fix/feat → strict; kind:refactor/chore → behavior-light;
89	// kind:doc/mechanical → mechanical. Defaults to strict when no kind label is
90	// present.
91	func BeadStrictnessMode(labels []string) StrictnessMode {
92		for _, l := range labels {
93			switch l {
94			case "kind:fix", "kind:feat":
95				return StrictnessStrict
96			case "kind:refactor", "kind:chore":
97				return StrictnessBehaviorLight
98			case "kind:doc", "kind:mechanical":
99				return StrictnessMechanical
100			}

100			}
101		}
102		return StrictnessStrict
103	}
104	
105	// ReviewResult is the structured outcome of a post-merge bead review.
106	type ReviewResult struct {
107		Verdict          Verdict    `json:"verdict"`
108		Rationale        string     `json:"rationale,omitempty"`
109		PerAC            []ReviewAC `json:"per_ac,omitempty"`
110		Findings         []Finding  `json:"findings,omitempty"`
111		ProseFindings    []Finding  `json:"prose_findings,omitempty"`
112		RawOutput        string     `json:"raw_output,omitempty"`
113		ReviewerHarness  string     `json:"reviewer_harness,omitempty"`
114		ReviewerModel    string     `json:"reviewer_model,omitempty"`
115		ReviewerProvider string     `json:"reviewer_provider,omitempty"`
116		ReviewerIndex    int        `json:"reviewer_index,omitempty"`
117		SessionID        string     `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
118		BaseRev          string     `json:"base_rev,omitempty"`
119		ResultRev        string     `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
120		ExecutionDir     string     `json:"execution_dir,omitempty"`
121		DurationMS       int        `json:"duration_ms,omitempty"`
122		Error            string     `json:"error,omitempty"`
123		CostUSD          float64    `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
124		// InputBytes and OutputBytes are the FEAT-022 §16 byte counters used to
125		// populate the compact summary appended to review and review-error event
126		// bodies. InputBytes is the assembled prompt size; OutputBytes is the
127		// raw reviewer output size (zero on pre-dispatch overflow / transport).
128		InputBytes  int `json:"input_bytes,omitempty"`
129		OutputBytes int `json:"output_bytes,omitempty"`
130	}
131	
132	type ReviewAC struct {
133		Number   int    `json:"number"`
134		Item     string `json:"item,omitempty"`
135		Grade    string `json:"grade,omitempty"`
136		Evidence string `json:"evidence,omitempty"`
137	}
138	
139	// ReviewGroupBundle identifies the shared evidence bundle used by a
140	// two-slot review-group dispatch.
141	type ReviewGroupBundle struct {

143		DirAbs    string `json:"dir_abs"`
144		DirRel    string `json:"dir_rel"`
145		PromptAbs string `json:"prompt_abs"`
146		PromptRel string `json:"prompt_rel"`

148	
149	// ReviewGroupSlotResult captures one slot's runtime and structured review
150	// result for a review-group dispatch.

359	func buildReviewPromptBounded(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef, opts BuildReviewPromptOptions, includeProse bool) BuildReviewPromptResult {
360		caps := opts.Caps
361		if caps.MaxPromptBytes == 0 {

363		}

371		beadStart := sb.Len()
372		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <bead id=%q iter=%d>\n", b.ID, iter)
373		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <title>%s</title>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.TrimSpace(b.Title)))
374	
375		if desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description); desc != "" {
376			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <description>\n%s\n    </description>\n", reviewXMLEscape(desc))
377		} else {

640		}
641		b.WriteString("[…file body omitted by ddx evidence cap…]\n")
642		return b.String()
643	}
644	
645	// beadReviewStrictnessSection returns a prompt section describing the strictness
646	// mode appropriate for the given mode. The section is appended to the
647	// instructions before dispatch.
648	func beadReviewStrictnessSection(mode StrictnessMode) string {
649		switch mode {
650		case StrictnessBehaviorLight:
651			return `
652	## Strictness mode: behavior-light (kind:refactor or kind:chore)
653	
654	For this bead, pass = build green + symbol/file evidence in the diff. You do not need to require test-name matches unless an AC explicitly names a Test* function. Focus on whether behavior is preserved, not on exhaustive test coverage.`
655		case StrictnessMechanical:
656			return `
657	## Strictness mode: mechanical (kind:doc or kind:mechanical)
658	
659	For this bead, pass = file presence, rename detection, or symbol presence in the diff. No behavioral or test evidence is required. Verify that the named files/symbols exist in the post-image.`
660		default: // StrictnessStrict
661			return `
662	## Strictness mode: strict (kind:fix or kind:feat)
663	
664	For this bead, each AC that names a Test* function must be confirmed by test evidence (test name present in diff or test output). Symbol and file evidence alone is insufficient for test-name ACs.`
665		}
666	}
667	
668	// mentionsPathLike returns true when text mentions the file path or its
669	// basename (a coarse heuristic — the reviewer only needs ranking, not
670	// exact symbolic resolution).
671	func mentionsPathLike(text, p string) bool {
672		if text == "" || p == "" {
673			return false
674		}
675		if strings.Contains(text, p) {
676			return true
677		}
678		base := path.Base(p)
679		return base != "" && base != "." && strings.Contains(text, base)
680	}
681	
682	// pathsRelated returns true when refPath and filePath share a directory
683	// prefix or basename, used to associate diff files with governing
684	// documents.
685	func pathsRelated(refPath, filePath string) bool {
686		if refPath == "" || filePath == "" {
687			return false
688		}
689		refDir := path.Dir(refPath)
690		if refDir != "" && refDir != "." && strings.HasPrefix(filePath, refDir+"/") {
691			return true
692		}
693		if path.Base(refPath) == path.Base(filePath) {
694			return true
695		}
696		return false
697	}
698	
699	// reviewXMLEscape escapes &, <, and > for inclusion in XML text content.
700	func reviewXMLEscape(s string) string {
701		var buf bytes.Buffer
702		buf.WriteString(strings.NewReplacer(
703			"&", "&",
704			"<", "<",
705			">", ">",
706		).Replace(s))
707		return buf.String()
708	}
709	
710	// DefaultBeadReviewer implements BeadReviewer by dispatching the review
711	// invocation through the agent service (or a test-injected runner).
712	// It fetches the bead, builds the review prompt, and runs the reviewer agent.
713	type DefaultBeadReviewer struct {
714		ProjectRoot string
715		BeadStore   BeadReader
716		// Service, when non-nil, is the agentlib.FizeauService used to dispatch the
717		// review invocation. Production callers leave this nil — ReviewBead
718		// constructs a fresh service from ProjectRoot via NewServiceFromWorkDir.
719		Service agentlib.FizeauService

20	// Git helpers

22	
23	// scrubbedGitEnvInteg returns the current environment with all GIT_* variables
24	// removed, ensuring test-local git subprocesses don't inherit parent repo state.
25	func scrubbedGitEnvInteg() []string {
26		parent := os.Environ()
27		env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
28		for _, kv := range parent {
29			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
30				continue
31			}
32			env = append(env, kv)
33		}
34		return env
35	}
36	
37	// runGitInteg runs a git command in dir with scrubbed GIT_* env.
38	// Fails the test on non-zero exit.
39	func runGitInteg(t *testing.T, dir string, args ...string) string {
40		t.Helper()
41		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
42		cmd.Dir = dir
43		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
44		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
45		if err != nil {
46			t.Fatalf("git %v in %s: %v\n%s", args, dir, err, out)
47		}
48		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
49	}
50	
51	// runGitIntegOutput runs a git command and returns (output, error) — for cases
52	// where failure is expected or handled by the caller.
53	func runGitIntegOutput(dir string, args ...string) (string, error) {
54		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
55		cmd.Dir = dir
56		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
57		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
58		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err
59	}

13	// seedBlocks appends `n` review-BLOCK events spaced 1 minute apart, starting
14	// at base. Returns the timestamp of the last BLOCK so test code can layer
15	// pairing-degraded events around it.
16	func seedBlocks(t *testing.T, store *bead.Store, beadID string, base time.Time, n int) time.Time {
17		t.Helper()
18		last := base
19		for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
20			last = base.Add(time.Duration(i) * time.Minute)
21			require.NoError(t, store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
22				Kind:      "review",
23				Summary:   "BLOCK",
24				Body:      "rationale",
25				Actor:     "reviewer",
26				Source:    "test",
27				CreatedAt: last,
28			}))
29		}
30		return last
31	}

42	// the caller. Report is the (possibly updated) ExecuteBeadReport; Approved
43	// reports whether the caller should treat this attempt as a success. When
44	// the review path encountered a non-fatal Store error the caller is expected
45	// to route StoreErr through its outcome-error handler
46	// (handleOutcomeStoreError on ExecuteBeadWorker), keyed by StoreErrOp.
47	//

49	// gap and exactly one repair cycle has been scheduled for this result_rev.
50	// The caller should run a repair attempt with MinPower above
51	// RepairContext.ImplementerActualPower while preserving all explicit
52	// harness/model/provider/profile pins from the original attempt.
53	type PostMergeReviewOutput struct {
54		Report        ExecuteBeadReport
55		Approved      bool

59	}

61	// RunPostMergeReview executes the post-merge review state machine for a
62	// successful bead attempt. When review is skipped (no reviewer or --no-review)
63	// the bead is closed and the function returns Approved=true. When review is
64	// active, the execute-loop asks the review coordinator to evaluate the
65	// close-eligible result before CloseWithEvidence runs; close only proceeds on
66	// unanimous evidence-backed APPROVE. REQUEST_CHANGES and BLOCK record their
67	// review events without reopening the bead.
68	//
69	// The review:skip label only skips when it carries a sibling
70	// review:skip-reason:* label; otherwise it is ignored. When review is not
71	// skipped, the review coordinator's verdict(s) or error drive the canonical
72	// event emissions (review / review-error / review-manual-required /
73	// review-malfunction), and on a non-APPROVE verdict the triage policy is
74	// still consulted without reopening the bead.
75	//
76	// Returns Approved=true on the APPROVE path and on the skip path; returns
77	// Approved=false on REQUEST_CHANGES, BLOCK, malformed APPROVE/BLOCK, or
78	// reviewer transport error. Store errors are surfaced via StoreErrOp +
79	// StoreErr so callers can keep the existing handleOutcomeStoreError pattern
80	// unchanged.

input.description: Find REQUEST_CLARIFICATION differences in golden test

data.output:
- REQUEST_CLARIFICATION — use ONLY when an ac-check item has result "needs_judgment" that you genuinely cannot adjudicate from the diff alone. This routes to the operator lane and does NOT block the queue.
        
        ## AC-check ratification
        
        When an <ac-check> section is present:
        - Ratify mechanical results (pass/fail) from the ac-check. Do not re-grep the diff for items the tool already resolved — accept them as-is unless you have concrete diff evidence of a mistake.
        - For "needs_judgment" items: examine the diff and choose pass or fail. Use REQUEST_CLARIFICATION only if the diff is genuinely insufficient to judge the AC.
        - To override a mechanical pass to BLOCK: you MUST add a judgment_overrides entry for that AC with a non-empty "reason" and a "diff_citation" pointing to the specific location in the diff.
        - When all mechanical ACs pass, you may APPROVE implicitly without re-verifying those items.
        
        ## Required output format (schema_version: 1)
        
--
        - "verdict" must be exactly one of "APPROVE", "REQUEST_CHANGES", "BLOCK", "REQUEST_CLARIFICATION".
        - "severity" must be exactly one of "info", "warn", "block".
        - "judgment_overrides" is required when overriding any mechanical ac-check pass result to BLOCK; omit when not overriding.
        - Output the JSON object inside ONE fenced ```json … ``` block. No additional prose, no extra fences, no markdown headings.
        - Do not echo this template back. Do not write the words APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES, BLOCK, or REQUEST_CLARIFICATION anywhere except as the JSON value of the verdict field.
        ## Strictness mode: behavior-light (kind:refactor or kind:chore)
